other_observers: Cole Wild; I am original finder
description: A gull slightly larger than accompanying Lesser Black-backed Gull (estimated 23 inches in length), but significantly smaller than accompanying immature Herring Gull. Head gray-brown, with darker smudge around eye, dark eye and smallish black bill. Mantle mottled brown and white, with a patch of gray feathers indicating freshly molted "first alternate" feathers. Underparts uniformly gray-brown like head. Wings heavily mottled brown and white. Tertials brown with several white bars across their center. Primaries concolorous with tertials (brown) with broad white edges. Pink legs and feet.Behaviors: Sleeping on ice, standing, preeningCall: nonePlumage: immature first cycle
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar
